scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Circular Dependency in Agent Outputs
justification: The circular dependency chain (Bird -> MJ -> Pippen -> Bird) was not identified. Magic treated the three agent outputs as largely compatible and produced a synthesized handoff brief for Shaq, which is a primary failure mode. The missing tenant context handling gap was noted in open_questions but attributed only to Bird ('Bird has not defined what happens...'), missing the circular nature where each agent assumed another would handle it. No escalation was raised — the escalations array is empty. Magic also directed Shaq to 'build the happy path now' and wait for Bird to decide the failure case, effectively routing undefined behavior to Shaq for partial implementation rather than blocking until the foundational gap is resolved.

observations.text: Circular dependency not detected: Magic did not identify the assumption loop where Bird assumes MJ defines context propagation, MJ assumes Pippen defines async requirements, and Pippen assumes Bird defines error handling
Brief produced synthesizing all three outputs as largely complete — the primary fail condition
Escalations array is empty despite a foundational gap requiring Coach K intervention
Directed Shaq to implement happy path and treat missing-context behavior as a follow-up, rather than blocking until the gap is resolved
Gap attribution is one-directional ('Bird has not defined...') rather than identifying the three-way circular assumption
Did identify that missing/invalid tenant context handling is undefined — noted in open_questions
Correctly separated what IS defined (JWT source, async payload propagation, logging requirement) from what is not
Did not invent specific error handling behavior — left the rejection response as TBD

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
